chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّ مِنَ السَّعَادَةِ أَنْ يَكُونَ مَعِيشَةُ الرَّجُلِ فِي بَلَدِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 121 – It is from the good fortune that there happens to be livelihood for the man in his own city

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/327)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/327)
text: قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ مِنْ سَعَادَةِ الْمَرْءِ أَنْ يَكُونَ مَتْجَرُهُ فِي بَلَدِهِ وَ يَكُونَ خُلَطَاؤُهُ صَالِحِينَ وَ يَكُونَ لَهُ وُلْدٌ يَسْتَعِينُ بِهِمْ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Usman Bin Isa, from Ibn Muskan, from one of his companions who said,

‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘From the good fortune of the person (are) that there happens to be his business in his own city, and his associates happen to be righteous, and there happen to be children for him he can seek assistance by them’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from ibn Muskan from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “Ali ibn Al-Husayn, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is of a man’s good fortune to have his place of business where he lives, to have virtuous associates and children who support him.’”
